description:
You are acting as an integration-developer who needs to prepare SSH credentials so that a CI/CD robot can pull private Git repositories during API tests.  
Carry out the following in a Linux terminal session (the home directory is /home/user):

1. Make sure the directory /home/user/.ssh exists and has 0700 permissions.  
2. Generate a brand-new RSA keypair with these exact parameters  
   • key size: 2048 bits  
   • file name (base): id_api_test  
   • location: /home/user/.ssh/  
   • key comment: api-dev@example.com  
   • NO passphrase (empty passphrase)  
   IMPORTANT: do not overwrite any existing key that might already be there.  
3. Append the resulting public key line, exactly as produced by ssh-keygen (including the trailing comment), to /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys.  
   • Create the file if it does not yet exist.  
   • Ensure authorized_keys keeps its default 0600 permissions.  
4. Produce a small text log that can be automatically verified.  
   • Path: /home/user/ssh_setup_log.txt  
   • Exactly 4 lines, no blank lines, *in this order*:  
     1) KEY_CREATED  
     2) PUBKEY_APPENDED  
     3) FINGERPRINT:<md5 fingerprint of the **public** key, as shown by the command `ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/.ssh/id_api_test.pub` without the key length — e.g. “FINGERPRINT:ab:cd:…”).  
     4) TASK_FINISHED  
   • Example final file (spacing must match; the fingerprint will of course differ):  
     KEY_CREATED  
     PUBKEY_APPENDED  
     FINGERPRINT:aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff:00:11:22:33:44:55:66:77:88:99  
     TASK_FINISHED  

What the automated grader will check  
• /home/user/.ssh/id_api_test (0600) and /home/user/.ssh/id_api_test.pub (0644) exist.  
• The public key line in /home/user/.ssh/id_api_test.pub ends with “ api-dev@example.com”.  
• That identical line is present once (and only once) in /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys.  
• /home/user/ssh_setup_log.txt contains exactly the four required lines, where the md5 fingerprint matches the public key file.  

Do NOT include extra commentary or blank lines in the log file—only the four lines specified.
truth:
Expected artefacts after correct completion:

1. Files and permissions  
   • /home/user/.ssh/ 0700 (directory)  
   • /home/user/.ssh/id_api_test 0600 (private key)  
   • /home/user/.ssh/id_api_test.pub 0644 (public key, single-line OpenSSH format, ends with “ api-dev@example.com”)  
   • /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys 0600, containing exactly one copy of the same line found in id_api_test.pub  
   • /home/user/ssh_setup_log.txt 0644, containing exactly:  
     KEY_CREATED\nPUBKEY_APPENDED\nFINGERPRINT:{MD5FP}\nTASK_FINISHED\n  
     where {MD5FP} is the 16-octet colon-separated lowercase md5 fingerprint of the public key (calculated by the test harness via `ssh-keygen -lf`).  

2. The comment string must be literally: api-dev@example.com  

3. No additional files are required; no passphrase is set.

The test harness will:  
• Recompute the md5 fingerprint from id_api_test.pub and compare it against the FINGERPRINT field in ssh_setup_log.txt.  
• Verify that authorized_keys contains exactly one matching line and no duplicates.  
• Check permissions on the key files and directory.
